title: 山核桃大街谋杀案
source_file: data\raw\detectiveqa\novel_data_en\121-山核桃大街谋杀案-阿加莎克里斯蒂.txt
summary:
Akibumbo suggests to the inspector that Miss Silvia’s death was staged to look like a suicide. He posits that a person living in the adjacent room could have jumped across the windows facing the chimney to enter her room. Mrs. Habberd identifies Nigel Bateson as the neighbor. Akibumbo reveals that Chandra Lal had previously reported that Nigel Bateson took his boric acid. This leads to the realization that someone took morphine from Nigel’s drawer, replaced it with boric acid, and Patricia Lane subsequently switched the boric acid for baking soda, mistakenly believing it was morphine. Sharp shows Mrs. Habberd two red strands of hair found in a package, which serves as a clue pointing toward Lennie Bateson.

Later, Hercule Poirot and Inspector Sharp discuss the case, noting that everything is now in order. Sharp prepares to go to 26 Walnut Street while Inspector Cobbe handles that location.

Inspector Cobbe and Detective Constable McRae arrive at a beauty salon in Mayfair owned by Mrs. Lucas to search the office of Miss Valerie Hobhouse. After searching the safe and desk, Cobbe discovers six blue leather-bound passports taped to the bottom of a desk drawer. The passports belong to various identities, including Mrs. Da Silva, Miss Irene Frank, Mrs. Olga Cohen, Miss Nina Le Meur, Mrs. Gladys Thomas, and Miss Moira O'Neill, all featuring photos of dark-skinned women using different hairstyles and disguises. Two additional foreign passports for Madame Mahamoudi and Miss Sheila Donovan are also found. Cobbe concludes that these identities were used for smuggling and money laundering through a Mayfair gambling club to avoid taxes, suggesting that Silvia may have seen one of these fake passports dropped on Mountbatten Street.
